sections.heading: Market Overview
sections.content: GCC markets opened mixed today. Saudi Tadawul edged up 0.3% on petrochemical gains, while Dubai Financial Market slipped 0.1% amid profit-taking. Abu Dhabi remained flat. Oil prices held near $85/bbl, supporting regional sentiment.

items.title: Saudi PIF Launches New Tourism Fund
items.summary: The Public Investment Fund announced a $5 billion fund to develop luxury resorts along the Red Sea coast, aiming to attract 150 million visitors by 2030.

items.title: UAE Non-Oil GDP Growth Accelerates
items.summary: UAE's non-oil GDP grew 5.2% YoY in Q1 2025, driven by tourism, real estate, and financial services, according to central bank data.

items.title: Qatar Energy Signs LNG Deal with Germany
items.summary: QatarEnergy inked a 15-year agreement to supply 2 million tonnes of LNG annually to German utility RWE, starting 2026.

sections.heading: Currency & Commodities
sections.content: The Saudi riyal and UAE dirham remained pegged to the USD. Gold edged up to $2,380/oz. Brent crude traded at $85.20/bbl, up 0.4% on Middle East supply concerns.

items.title: ADNOC Drilling Wins $1.2B Contract
items.summary: ADNOC Drilling secured a $1.2 billion contract for offshore drilling services in Abu Dhabi's Zakum field.

items.title: Kuwait's NBK Reports Record Profit
items.summary: National Bank of Kuwait posted a record net profit of $1.8 billion for 2024, up 12% YoY, driven by higher net interest income.

sections.heading: Outlook
sections.content: Markets are expected to remain range-bound today as investors await US inflation data. GCC fiscal spending and reform momentum continue to support medium-term growth.
